ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for grilled scallops with cheese specifically; FOODON_03315595 (scallop food product) used as the closest parent anchor. Cheese-topped grilled preparation is not individually coded in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers scallops as a commodity; no specific code exists for the grilled-with-cheese preparation. Used as the closest defensible parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for grilled scallops with cheese dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 4 grilled scallops with cheese topping, approximately 160 g total, typically served as a starter or sharing dish.

nutrient_source.title: Grilled Scallops With Cheese (Sò Điệp Nướng Phô Mai) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Grilled Scallops With Cheese (Sò Điệp Nướng Phô Mai), CR ID G00010584, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite (processed cheese + scallop + butter) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on mixed seafood-cheese dish with low carbohydrate content
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for grilled scallops with cheese. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on the analogous category of mixed protein-fat-dominant dishes with low carbohydrate content (carbohydrate 8.85 g/100g, predominantly from cheese lactose and minor starch). Protein and fat content strongly attenuate glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (35) × carbohydrate per serving (8.85 g/100g × 1.6 serving = 14.16 g) / 100 = 5.0 g. University of Sydney GI Database consulted; no exact match found.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1279 mg/100g — more than double the 600 mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods; processed cheese is the primary driver
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request reduced cheese or substitute fresh mozzarella, avoid dipping sauces, and account for this dish contributing more than half the recommended daily sodium intake (2000 mg) in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 8.85 g/100g and sugar 6.24 g/100g are below thresholds, but high saturated fat (~13.5 g/100g) from processed cheese worsens insulin resistance over time
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate and glycemic load make this dish acceptable for blood glucose control in the short term, but the high saturated fat content from processed cheese should be limited to avoid worsening insulin resistance; request reduced cheese and avoid sweet chili dipping sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at ~13.5 g/100g and sodium at 1279 mg/100g both exceed cardiovascular-safe thresholds; cholesterol at 78 mg/100g adds additional atherogenic load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high risk for cardiovascular disease patients due to combined saturated fat, sodium, and cholesterol burden; scallops themselves are cardioprotective (omega-3, lean protein) but the cheese and butter preparation negates this benefit — request minimal cheese and butter-free grilling.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~13.5 g/100g far exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold; sodium 1279 mg/100g worsens RA-related tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in its standard preparation; scallops are inherently anti-inflammatory but the processed cheese and butter topping drives pro-inflammatory saturated fat and sodium to levels that trigger cytokine activity — request grilled scallops with minimal seasoning and no cheese.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Scallops with Cheese (Vietnamese: Sò Điệp Nướng Phô Mai) is a popular Vietnamese street food and restaurant dish consisting of fresh scallops grilled in their shells and topped with a generous layer of melted processed cheese, typically combined with butter, garlic, spring onion, and chili. The dish is served hot, with the cheese forming a golden, slightly caramelised crust over the tender, sweet scallop meat. The texture contrast between the firm, juicy scallop and the rich, creamy melted cheese is a defining characteristic. In Singapore, this dish is commonly found in Vietnamese restaurants and food courts, often listed under its Vietnamese name. Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein (16.09 g/100g) from the scallops, high in fat (23.06 g/100g) predominantly from processed cheese and butter, and very high in sodium (1279 mg/100g) and calcium (1375 mg/100g) from the cheese. The carbohydrate content is low (8.85 g/100g), giving it a low glycemic index and glycemic load. Clinically, the dish is unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients due to its extreme sodium and saturated fat content, and should be consumed with caution by individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its saturated fat load. Scallops themselves are a source of lean protein, zinc, selenium, and omega-3 fatty acids, but the cheese-heavy preparation significantly alters the nutritional profile. A standard serving is 4 pieces, approximately 160 g.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID G00010584, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analysis of the known ingredients (processed cheese, scallops, butter) and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct measurement exists for this dish.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (13.5 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central ingredient-level data for processed cheese (USDA FDC #170848), scallops (USDA FDC #175177), and butter (USDA FDC #173410) weighted by approximate recipe proportions, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this dish and are not available from a single approved source for this specific preparation as of 2026. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as confirmed by HPB source; scallops contain no dietary fibre and the cheese topping contributes negligible fibre. 4. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Fish and seafood' as the dish is a shellfish preparation; the original classification appears to be a data entry error. 5. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Halal status not confirmed — processed cheese source and preparation method not verified for halal compliance. 7. Ontology match types set to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 as no exact code exists for this specific grilled-scallops-with-cheese preparation; this is within the allowed values of ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].
